title: Quercus mindanaensis
taxonomy: Quercus mindanaensis is classified as a species within the genus Quercus, which is a group of deciduous and evergreen trees commonly known as oak trees. The genus Quercus belongs to the family Fagaceae, which is a group of flowering plants that are commonly known as the beech family. The Fagaceae family is part of the order Fagales, a group of flowering plants that are often found in temperate regions. The order Fagales is part of the clade Rosid, which is a large group of flowering plants that are often found in a variety of environments. The Rosid clade is part of the larger clade of flowering plants known as the angiosperms.
uses: Quercus mindanaensis, also known as the Mindanao oak, is a species of oak tree native to the Philippines. There is limited information available about its medicinal uses, but traditionally, various parts of the plant have been used in folk medicine to treat different health issues. In some regions, the leaves and bark of Quercus mindanaensis are used to make tea, which is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
